key_insight: MCH-Guard is a multimodal machine-learning framework that predicts cerebral microhemorrhage risk and stability in ADNI, with strong performance even from clinical history alone.
clinical_relevance: This could help prioritize microhemorrhage and ARIA-H surveillance for anti-amyloid therapy by identifying higher-risk patients earlier and more equitably.
method: Nested models combined demographics, clinical history, fluid biomarkers, and imaging.
The framework predicted baseline MCH presence, incident MCH, time-to-onset, and four-year risk.
A transient vascular instability phenotype was also modeled and linked to hepatic factors.
evidence_signal: In ADNI (N=813), the comprehensive model reached AUC 0.86 for baseline MCH detection, while the minimal clinical-only model still achieved AUC 0.82 and longitudinal prediction reached R2=0.68.
watch_item: Performance was evaluated in a single dataset, so external validation and careful interpretation are needed before real-world deployment.
paper_title: MCH-Guard: Multimodal Machine Learning Framework for Risk Stratification of Cerebral Microhemorrhage Risk in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative
